question:
GaryClaw on main branch. Your working tree has uncommitted changes from the eng review (modified files in src/cli.ts, src/orchestrator.ts, docs/designs/brain-first-roadmap.md, plus new test files). /qa needs a clean tree so each bug fix gets its own atomic commit. How should we handle these?

RECOMMENDATION: Choose A because uncommitted work from the eng review should be preserved as a commit before QA adds its own fix commits. Completeness: A=10/10, B=7/10, C=3/10.

options.label: Commit my changes (Recommended)
options.description: Commit all current changes with a descriptive message, then start QA

options.label: Stash my changes
options.description: Stash changes, run QA, pop the stash after

options.label: Abort
options.description: I'll clean up manually and re-run /qa

rationale: Preserving eng review work as an atomic commit before QA ensures completeness — nothing gets lost, and QA gets the clean tree it needs for its own atomic fix commits.
